enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs a Friday in Boulder where he spent the day doing detailed ski resort mapping work in Google Earth and Dreamweaver, tracing runs and lifts for Breckenridge. He describes a time-consuming snafu with missing and incorrectly traced ski runs, discusses his interest in making and honing project-completion predictions as a skill and untapped YouTube genre, and notes his terrible home internet preventing a live demo.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: genuinely enjoys it, sees accurate prediction as a hone-able skill and an undiscovered YouTube genre
excited — it's his first project he has done fully end-to-end and is actively predicting completion within the next week

transcript.text: Hey, Living in Boulder Vlog of October 28th, 2016.

I'm not going to work tomorrow.
It's going to be the first time I've been working on a Saturday for a while.
So what happened today was I actually kind of got up late.
It's fun to do videos.

I'll tell you about today's snafu.

And then, so I make the connectors, the links for Google or Dreamweaver to connect to for each of those.
And it's 173 for Breckenridge.

So I restore it, bring it up.
I see the name and I put the name on the lift, but then the, the run under the lift is not there.

It's not there, right?
Like you look at the whole ski resort and only there's a missing rung, you know?
So I just didn't see it.
So I say, I, I trace it in and out of 173 ski resorts, this one's going to be like 73, which is like top half, well under the top half.
And it seems like it's always like that.
It's like at the top.
And then I see a couple other ones that are just traced wrong.
I just didn't notice.
So I trace them, right?
